text.title: LLM-Friendly Architecture Anti-Patterns
text.summary: Five anti-patterns that degrade AI edit accuracy and three agent-specific failure modes unique to AI-assisted refactoring workflows.

antipattern.description: God component: single file over 800 lines combining routing, auth, data fetching, state, calculations, and rendering.
antipattern.reason: Context truncation causes the agent to miss the lower half of the file. Multiple concerns mean any change risks unintended side effects.

antipattern.description: Utils dumping ground: utils/index.ts with 50+ unrelated functions.
antipattern.reason: Agents cannot infer which function to edit without reading the entire file. New functions get added to the dump rather than purpose-named files.

antipattern.description: Barrel re-exports: components/index.ts with export * from './Button' for 20+ components.
antipattern.reason: Agents edit the barrel as if it is the implementation. Circular dependency risk increases. TypeScript path alias cache may mask broken imports after restructuring.

antipattern.description: Inline large data: 200+ lines of JSON or data arrays inside a component function body.
antipattern.reason: Inflates the file past the point where the agent holds the full rendering logic in context. Data line edits corrupt adjacent rendering code.

antipattern.description: Ambiguous file names: helpers.ts, common.ts, shared.ts, misc.ts as catch-all files.
antipattern.reason: Agents search by filename. Multiple ambiguous candidates cause wrong-file edits that produce no-ops or bugs in unrelated modules.

rule.statement: After any agent-driven file split, explicitly verify the original code is removed. Diff original against new files before committing.
rule.rationale: Agents executing multi-step refactors lose track of deletion steps when context fills. The review pass must check for duplicate code explicitly.

rule.statement: After a large restructure, explicitly update tsconfig.json path aliases. Run tsc --noEmit in CI to catch stale paths.
rule.rationale: Agents moving files frequently miss tsconfig changes. Stale path aliases compile locally with cached resolution but fail in CI.

rule.statement: Run madge --circular in CI after any agent-driven restructure to detect circular imports introduced by the split.
rule.rationale: New circular imports from file splits are not caught by tsc or eslint by default. Madge is the standard tool for this check.
